name: Copenhagen
summary: Copenhagen is the capital of Denmark, a city of canals, castles and bicycles. It is the home of LEGO and of Hans Christian Andersen, the storyteller who wrote The Little Mermaid and The Ugly Duckling.
funFacts: Tivoli Gardens opened in 1843, making it one of the oldest amusement parks in the world, and it inspired Walt Disney when he was dreaming up Disneyland.
Copenhageners own about five times more bicycles than cars, and on busy days more bikes than cars roll through the city centre.
LEGO bricks were invented in Denmark in 1932, and the name comes from two Danish words, 'leg godt', which mean 'play well'.
The Round Tower has a curving brick ramp inside instead of stairs, wide enough that a horse and carriage can spiral all the way to the top.
The Little Mermaid statue is much smaller than people expect, only about 1.25 metres tall, sitting on a rock right by the harbour.
thingsToDo: Spin through Tivoli Gardens and ride the wooden roller coaster from 1914, one of the oldest still running anywhere.
Hop on a canal boat from Nyhavn, gliding past the rainbow row of old sailor houses by the water.
Walk along the harbour to meet the Little Mermaid statue, then explore the star-shaped Kastellet fortress nearby.
Climb the Round Tower's curving brick ramp up to a rooftop platform with sweeping views over the old city.
Visit Rosenborg Castle to peek at Denmark's real crown jewels, then run around the King's Garden park outside.
Cross the bridge to Christiansborg Palace, where the Queen welcomes guests and you can climb the tallest tower in town.

wildlife.name: Red Deer
wildlife.description: Denmark's biggest wild animal, with rusty-brown fur and huge branching antlers on the stags.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Plump grey-spotted seal that pops its whiskery head out of the water around Danish coasts.

wildlife.name: Mute Swan
wildlife.description: Denmark's national bird, a snowy-white swan with an orange beak and graceful curved neck.

wildlife.name: White-tailed Eagle
wildlife.description: Huge brown eagle with a snowy white tail, soaring over Danish lakes and forests.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Danish, used by everyone, all day long.

images.alt: Rainbow-coloured townhouses line the Nyhavn canal with wooden sailing boats moored along the water.
Tivoli Gardens glows with thousands of fairy lights reflected on a calm lake at blue hour.
The Little Mermaid bronze statue sitting on her rock by Copenhagen's harbour.
The red-brick Renaissance towers of Rosenborg Castle rise above its leafy royal gardens.
Copenhagen's 17th-century Round Tower stands tall above the cobbled streets of the old city.
Christiansborg Palace seen across the canal, with its tall green spire rising above the rooftops.
A herd of red deer grazing in the Lille Vildmose nature reserve in Denmark.
A whiskery harbour seal resting on a rock with water all around.
A snowy-white mute swan in flight with its long neck stretched forward.
A white-tailed eagle gliding low over the water with wings spread wide.
